page: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-90 is a memory-optimized server powered by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 90 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 45 physical cores with multithreading enabled. The system is equipped with 720.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, requiring external network storage solutions for persistent data.

Benchmark results indicate top-tier multi-core performance, with stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and software compilation benchmarks ranking in the top 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is strong, reaching 6374.57 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 218.2 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, though large-block write bandwidth is average at 77.77 GB/sec. The processor supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, contributing to top-tier performance in Redis SET operations and prompt processing for small language models.

Given its high memory density and dedicated AMD EPYC compute resources, this server offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that require substantial RAM without the added cost of local NVMe storage or GPU accelerators. This configuration is highly suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching systems like Redis, software compilation pipelines, and memory-intensive data processing tasks.
description: Google Cloud Platform c3d-highmem-90 is a memory-optimized server featuring 90 dedicated vCPUs and 720.0 GB of RAM, driven by an AMD EPYC 9B14 processor on the x86_64 architecture. With 45 physical cores and multithreading, it delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, ranking in the top 10% on stress-ng and PassMark benchmarks. The server provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, supporting strong read memory bandwidth up to 6374.57 GB/sec. It contains no local storage or GPUs, making it a specialized resource for memory-intensive workloads. The hardware supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and cryptography acceleration, enabling high-throughput Redis operations and efficient text processing. This instance is best suited for large-scale database operations, in-memory caching, and memory-heavy enterprise applications.

tagline: A high-density memory-optimized server featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors for demanding database and in-memory computing workloads.
